target.name: Zeta Surgical
target.founder: Jose Amich, Raahil Sha, William Gormley

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $100M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Zeta Surgical is a Series A-stage biotech company founded by Jose Amich, Raahil Sha, and William Gormley, having raised $23M to date against a current post-money valuation of $100M. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 8 private-stage peers in Biotech & Health (including Eikon Therapeutics, Xaira Therapeutics, Neko Health, New Limit, and Asimov) and 3 public biotech benchmarks (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

Two triangulation methods apply here. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $100M last post-money and applying a typical 2.0x Series A-to-Series B step-up multiplier, implies a $200M valuation. Peer-median analysis across the 6 same-sector comparables at matching or adjacent stage yields a $1.3B median, with a range of $600M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring via EV/Sales multiples (6.1x median in the sector) cannot be applied due to absence of revenue data for the private target. The peer set spans valuations from $800M to $3.3B, reflecting wide dispersion in the frontier biotech segment.

Synthesizing the two applicable methods produces a triangulated range of $170M (low) to $1.5B (high), with a midpoint of $752M. The current $100M valuation sits materially below the low end, suggesting upside potential, though substantial uncertainty persists around revenue trajectory, clinical progress, and whether peer valuations reflect sustainable market multiples or frothy conditions in early-stage biotech.
